name: rung_431_book_goldilocks_raw_chaptered_qa
utterances: Use predicates bear/1, goldilocks/1, tasted/2, ate_all/2, broke/1, slept_in/2, saw/2, porridge_of/2, chair_of/2, bed_of/2 for this story when possible.

Chapter chunk 1: Once upon a time there were three Bears, who lived together in a house of their own, in a wood. One was a Little Wee Bear, one was a Middle-sized Bear, and the other was a Great Big Bear. They had a bowl for each bear, a chair for each bear, and a bed for each bear.
Great Big Bear is a bear.
Middle-sized Bear is a bear.
Little Wee Bear is a bear.
Goldilocks was a little girl.

Chapter chunk 3: First Goldilocks tasted the porridge of the Great Big Bear and it was too hot. Next she tasted the porridge of the Middle-sized Bear and it was too cold. Then she tasted the porridge of the Little Wee Bear and it was just right, so she ate it all.
Goldilocks tasted Great Big Bear's porridge.
Goldilocks tasted Middle-sized Bear's porridge.
Goldilocks tasted Little Wee Bear's porridge.
Goldilocks ate all of Little Wee Bear's porridge.
Chapter chunk 4: Goldilocks sat in the great chair, then in the middle-sized chair, and then in the little chair. The little chair broke while she sat in it.
Little Wee Bear's chair broke.
Chapter chunk 5: Goldilocks went upstairs and tried the three beds. The little bed was just right and she fell asleep there.
Goldilocks slept in Little Wee Bear's bed.
Chapter chunk 6: The three bears returned, looked around, and then went upstairs. Little Wee Bear found Goldilocks still in his bed.
Little Wee Bear saw Goldilocks in his bed.
Who ate all the porridge?
Which chair broke?
Who saw Goldilocks in a bed?
